operating_state: Net sales declined 2.3% to $36.4 billion driven by negative comparable sales of 2.8%, reflecting decreased customer transactions and average ticket amid macroeconomic pressures including consumer shift from goods to services and high interest rate impacts on home improvement demand. Building Materials and Power departments posted positive comparable sales while other merchandising departments declined. Online sales grew 3.3% and represented 15.3% of net sales.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin expanded 40 basis points to 34.1% driven by lower transportation costs and reduced shrink, offsetting the sales decline. Operating expenses increased due to higher payroll costs, lower legal-related benefits, and deleverage from negative comparable sales, with depreciation rising from ongoing business investments and sales deleverage.
material_events: Completed $18.25 billion acquisition of SRS Distribution on June 18, 2024, establishing the company as a leading specialty trade distributor serving professional roofers, landscapers and pool contractors. Issued $10 billion in senior notes across multiple maturities on June 25, 2024 to fund the acquisition. Increased quarterly dividend 7.7% to $2.25 per share in February 2024.
macro_exposures: High interest rate environment continues pressuring home improvement demand and customer transaction volumes. Consumer spending patterns shifted away from goods toward services, creating headwinds for retail operations. Foreign exchange provided modest positive impact from weaker U.S. dollar during the quarter.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures of $3.0-3.5 billion for fiscal 2024, approximately 2% of net sales, focused on new stores and customer experience improvements including technology investments. SRS acquisition expected to accelerate growth with residential professional customers and complement existing capabilities for complex project purchase occasions. Share repurchases paused in March 2024 and will not resume until excess cash reduces outstanding debt following the acquisition.
uncertainty_profile: SRS acquisition integration risks and realization of anticipated synergies across specialty trade distribution verticals remain unresolved. Commercial paper program expanded to $19.5 billion with corresponding backup credit facilities to support acquisition financing, creating elevated debt service obligations and operational integration execution challenges.
